generation.method: checked runtime inventory plus exact npm tarballs extracted into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per scenario and mode

probeModes.reason: Default Node development records public errors and development-only diagnostics.
Default Node production records minified public errors and production return shapes.
The react-server condition removes the form-action root APIs and makes server rendering unavailable.
Production react-server behavior is recorded separately for condition-specific absence and throws.

scenarios.expectedBoundary: Default React DOM exposes form APIs as writable enumerable functions; the react-server condition omits them.
Rootless and non-React-owned form-like values all throw the public invalid-form error instead of silently resetting.
Calling form hooks outside render throws through the null hook dispatcher; development also emits the invalid-hook diagnostic.
Server rendering exposes the non-pending form status object shape without exercising client form submission.
Server rendering exposes the action-state tuple shape without dispatching an action.
Server rendering a function form action emits React's submit-replay boundary while status remains not pending.

args.value:
Invalid hook call. Hooks can only be called inside of the body of a function component. This could happen for one of the following reasons:
1. You might have mismatching versions of React and the renderer (such as React DOM)
2. You might be breaking the Rules of Hooks
3. You might have more than one copy of React in the same app
See https://react.dev/link/invalid-hook-call for tips about how to debug and fix this problem.

Invalid hook call. Hooks can only be called inside of the body of a function component. This could happen for one of the following reasons:
1. You might have mismatching versions of React and the renderer (such as React DOM)
2. You might be breaking the Rules of Hooks
3. You might have more than one copy of React in the same app
See https://react.dev/link/invalid-hook-call for tips about how to debug and fix this problem.

result.label: server render function form action boundary

html.value: <form action="javascript:throw new Error('React form unexpectedly submitted.')"><button type="submit" value="save" name="intent">save</button></form><script>addEventListener("submit",function(a){if(!a.defaultPrevented){var c=a.target,d=a.submitter,e=c.action,b=d;if(d){var f=d.getAttribute("formAction");null!=f&&(e=f,b=null)}"javascript:throw new Error('React form unexpectedly submitted.')"===e&&(a.preventDefault(),b?(a=document.createElement("input"),a.name=b.name,a.value=b.value,b.parentNode.insertBefore(a,b),b=new FormData(c),a.parentNode.removeChild(a)):b=new FormData(c),a=c.ownerDocument||c,(a.$$reactFormReplay=a.$$reactFormReplay||[]).push(c,d,b))}});</script>
html.startsWith: <form action="javascript:throw new Error('React form unexpectedly submitted.')"><button type="submit" value="s

intentionalGaps.reason: This oracle records pinned React DOM behavior only; Fast React React DOM placeholders and implementation are owned by separate workers.
requestFormReset success requires a React-owned live DOM form, reconciler host instances, and client commit state. This oracle intentionally covers rootless and non-React-owned invalid inputs only.
The probes do not dispatch browser submit/reset events, mutate real FormData, or observe a pending host transition. They only capture server-render output and non-pending hook shapes.
The checked React DOM export oracle already records that profiling exposes these APIs. This oracle focuses on the root form API behavior surface.
